body:
Title: CRA Form NT428 and Worksheet NT428 2025 Northwest Territories resident tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-c/5012-c-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-c/5012-c-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-d/5012-d-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5012-d/5012-d-25e.pdf

NT428 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:

CRA Form NT428 2025 calculates Northwest Territories tax for residents. Part A calculates tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $51,964, $103,930, and $168,967. The rates are 5.9%, 8.6%, 12.2%, and 14.05%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $3,065.88, $7,534.95, and $15,469.47.

Part B calculates Northwest Territories non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $17,842. Worksheet NT428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $8,727, starts reducing above $45,522, is zero at $103,702 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $17,842. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $14,469 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,834 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 5.9%. Donations and gifts line 58969 multiplies federal Schedule 9 line 13 by 5.9% and line 14 by 14.05%. Line 61500 adds line 58840 and line 58969 as Northwest Territories non-refundable tax credits.

Part C calculates Northwest Territories tax. Line 54 adds tax on taxable income and tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet NT428 line 61520 calculates the dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 6% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 11.5%. Lines 57 and 60 multiply minimum tax amounts by 45%. Line 63 subtracts territorial foreign tax credit. Worksheet NT428 line 65 calculates the political contribution credit: contributions of $100 or less are credited dollar for dollar, contributions of $900 or more receive $500, and otherwise line 65 equals $100 plus 50% of contributions above $100, capped at $500. Line 66 enters Northwest Territories tax on line 42800.
